name: Honduran Mahogany
description: A reference tonewood prized for its warm midrange, sustain, and complex resonance. Each blank is quarter-sawn from old-growth stock, dried slowly in Arizona's arid climate, and hand-selected for grain consistency. Recommended for hollow-bodied builds and instruments intended to age into voice.

name: Eastern Hard Maple
description: Dense, bright, and articulate. Selected for figured grain — flame, quilt, or birdseye — and resawn to expose the most expressive face. Pairs with darker tonewoods for laminated bodies; stands alone for instruments requiring clarity and attack.

name: Black Limba
description: West African hardwood with a striking dark-veined figure over a pale honey field. Tonally balanced — open midrange, defined low end. Reserved for one-off builds; supply is limited and selected only when grain and density meet our standard.

name: Swamp Ash
description: Lightweight, resonant, and visually understated. Open-grained with a chambered cellular structure that produces a pronounced attack and singing sustain. The traditional choice for vintage-style solid bodies.

name: Spanish Cedar
description: Aromatic, lightweight, and tonally warm. Long favored in classical and acoustic construction; offered here as a solid-body alternative for players seeking woody warmth without weight. Limited release.

name: White Korina
description: The pale counterpart to Black Limba. A vintage signature wood — light, resonant, and tonally elegant with a slightly scooped midrange. Reserved for builders seeking historical authenticity.
